SEARCH
Package org.apache.arrow.datafusion.datasource

Class ListingTableConfig

java.lang.Object
org.apache.arrow.datafusion.datasource.ListingTableConfig
public class ListingTableConfig extends Object
Configuration for creating a ListingTable.

A listing table config specifies the paths, options, and schema for a file-backed table. Use the single-path constructor for one directory, or the multi-path constructor for multiple directories.

Example: 

ListingTableUrl url = ListingTableUrl.parse("/path/to/data/");
ListingOptions options = ListingOptions.builder(myFormat).build();
ListingTableConfig config = new ListingTableConfig(url)
    .withListingOptions(options)
    .withSchema(mySchema);
ListingTable table = new ListingTable(config);
See Also:
  • Rust DataFusion: ListingTableConfig

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    ListingTableConfig(List<ListingTableUrl> tablePaths)
    Creates a config for multiple table paths.
    ListingTableConfig(ListingTableUrl tablePath)
    Creates a config for a single table path.

  • Constructor Details

    • ListingTableConfig

      public ListingTableConfig(ListingTableUrl tablePath)
      Creates a config for a single table path.
      Parameters:
      tablePath - the directory URL
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: ListingTableConfig::new

    • ListingTableConfig

      public ListingTableConfig(List<ListingTableUrl> tablePaths)
      Creates a config for multiple table paths.
      Parameters:
      tablePaths - the directory URLs
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: ListingTableConfig::new_with_multi_paths

  • Method Details

    • withListingOptions

      public ListingTableConfig withListingOptions(ListingOptions options)
      Sets the listing options.

      Example: 

      ListingOptions options = ListingOptions.builder(myFormat)
    .collectStat(true)
    .build();
ListingTableConfig config = new ListingTableConfig(url)
    .withListingOptions(options);
      Parameters:
      options - the listing options
      Returns:
      this config
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: ListingTableConfig::with_listing_options

    • withSchema

      public ListingTableConfig withSchema(org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.Schema schema)
      Sets the table schema.

      Example: 

      Schema schema = new Schema(List.of(
    Field.nullable("id", new ArrowType.Int(32, true)),
    Field.nullable("name", ArrowType.Utf8.INSTANCE)));
ListingTableConfig config = new ListingTableConfig(url)
    .withSchema(schema);
      Parameters:
      schema - the Arrow schema
      Returns:
      this config
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: ListingTableConfig::with_schema

    • tablePaths

      public List<ListingTableUrl> tablePaths()
      Returns the table paths.

      Example: 

      ListingTableConfig config = new ListingTableConfig(url);
List<ListingTableUrl> paths = config.tablePaths();
for (ListingTableUrl path : paths) {
    System.out.println(path.getUrl());
}
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: ListingTableConfig::table_paths

    • listingOptions

      public ListingOptions listingOptions()
      Returns the listing options, or null if not yet set.

      Example: 

      ListingTableConfig config = new ListingTableConfig(url)
    .withListingOptions(options);
ListingOptions opts = config.listingOptions();
FileFormat format = opts.format();
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: ListingTableConfig::options

    • schema

      public org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.Schema schema()
      Returns the table schema, or null if not yet set.

      Example: 

      ListingTableConfig config = new ListingTableConfig(url)
    .withSchema(mySchema);
Schema schema = config.schema();
List<Field> fields = schema.getFields();
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: ListingTableConfig::file_schema
